sources.name: GameFAQs — Rocket Knight Adventures Guide
sources.notes: Confirms: 8-directional rocket burst, hold A/C to charge, release to launch. Wall rebounding mechanic confirmed.

notes: DEFINING MECHANIC: Sparkster's rocket pack. 8-directional rocket burst that can be aimed and wall-rebounded.
Rocket burst is CHARGED by holding the attack button — fills a gauge, then releases all at once.
The 8-directional burst means Sparkster can move in any direction, including upward and diagonally.
Wall rebound: rocket burst into a wall → bounce off the wall in an opposite direction, continuing the trajectory.
Walk speed and jump values not explicitly documented in reviewed sources — estimated from gameplay and Genesis standards.
Sparkster is an anthropomorphic opossum — he can use his prehensile tail in some moves.

standard_jump.notes: Standard jump before rocket mechanics. VERIFY.

rocket_pack._note: THE DEFINING MECHANIC. The rocket pack is a directional charged burst with wall rebounding.

charge_system.description: Hold A or C (attack button) to charge the rocket gauge. A blue bar on the HUD fills while held.
charge_system.activation: Release the attack button when gauge is full to fire the rocket burst.
notes: The charge must fill to maximum before the rocket activates — partial charges don't fire.
While charging, Sparkster can still be hit and must maintain the hold.
The charge time is approximately 1.5-2 seconds at normal speed.

burst_direction.description: 8-directional rocket burst: up, upper-right, right, lower-right, down, lower-left, left, upper-left. Direction determined by D-pad held at moment of release.

burst_speed.notes: High-speed burst — significantly faster than walking or running. VERIFY exact value.

wall_rebound.description: When the rocket burst trajectory hits a wall, Sparkster rebounds off in the reflected direction.
notes: Wall rebounding allows chaining rocket bursts off multiple walls to reach elevated platforms.
This creates a movement language unique to RKA — 'billiard ball' style pathing through vertical areas.
Players can plan multi-wall routes to reach otherwise inaccessible areas.
The rebound bounces at the wall's normal angle — off a flat wall: perfect reflection.

collateral_damage.description: Rocket burst destroys lesser enemies in Sparkster's path during the burst.
collateral_damage.notes: The burst itself is an attack as well as a movement tool.

sword_attack.description: Sparkster's sword — melee attack. Used for standard enemies and basic combat.
sword_attack.notes: The sword is the non-charged attack. Rocket burst is the charged attack.

sparkster_genesis.change: Rocket pack charges AUTOMATICALLY. Separate Rocket button instead of hold-to-charge.
sparkster_genesis.notes: The sequel (Sparkster: Rocket Knight Adventures 2) significantly changes the charge mechanic, making the rocket more accessible but less tactical.

design_notes_for_giants_drink: The charge-and-release rocket burst creates a rhythm of preparation and payoff. Players charge during safe moments and release at critical moments (clearing a gap, crossing a hazard). The charge time creates tension — 'can I hold this long enough before I get hit?'

The 8-directional burst + wall rebound combination creates a traversal vocabulary unique in the platformer genre. Players solve vertical navigation like a billiard puzzle: 'which wall do I hit first to reach the top?' This is spatial reasoning rather than timing.
The burst destroying enemies in path means the movement tool is also a combat tool — the same action handles traversal AND combat, avoiding the 'move here, then fight' loop of other games.

Wall rebounding off multiple surfaces chains into complex routes that feel spectacular when executed correctly. This is 'flow state' design: mastery produces chains of rebound → fly → rebound → fly that feel controlled and elegant.
The rocket pack as a movement system is explicitly 3-dimensional thinking applied to 2D: you need to reason about launch angle, wall angles, and landing zones before committing to the burst.
